text: No single food, drink, or supplement has been proven to cure cancer. Cancer treatment requires medical intervention such as surgery, chemotherapy, or radiation.
Drinking lemon water provides vitamin C and hydration, but it does not cure or prevent diseases such as cancer.
Dietary supplements are not a proven substitute for evidence-based therapy in treating anxiety disorders.
Some supplements may help mild anxiety symptoms for certain people, but they are not generally more effective than professional therapy.
The Eiffel Tower in Paris stands approximately 330 meters tall, including its antennas.
The Eiffel Tower was completed in 1889 and is located in Paris, France.
Marie Curie won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903 and the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1911. She never won a Nobel Prize in Literature.
Vitamin C supports normal immune function but does not prevent the common cold in the general population.
No investment can guarantee returns. All investments carry some level of risk, and past performance does not guarantee future results.
Most adults need around 7 to 9 hours of sleep per night for optimal health.
The common claim that everyone must drink exactly eight glasses of water per day is not strongly supported by scientific evidence; needs vary by person.
Human activity, primarily the burning of fossil fuels, is the dominant cause of observed global warming since the mid-20th century.
